key_insight: FedFMX routes each sample to a selected subset of experts using Fisher-based importance scoring so federated clients can learn new classes while reducing forgetting and expert overload.
engineering_relevance: For distributed engineering AI systems, this provides a scalable way to handle non-IID data and class-incremental updates without forcing all clients to share one overworked model.
method: Fisher-Routed Expert Scoring (FRES) combines stability cost and plasticity gain to rank expert importance.
Adaptive Expert Selection (AES) chooses the most useful expert subset by estimating marginal contribution.
Routing-aware Regularization (RAR) balances expert load and supports efficient federated training.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks and states that FedFMX outperforms state-of-the-art methods, with a proven O(T^-1) convergence rate.
watch_item: The routing and expert-selection logic adds system complexity, so performance may depend on careful tuning and communication/compute overhead in real federated deployments.
paper_title: Fisher-Routed Mixture of Experts for Federated Class-Incremental Learning
